payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it is the newer, current memory for Sam from Goldman. mem_old was filtered out because it is superseded, so it should not be treated as the current fact.
citations: input: 'Explain why recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman and filtered mem_old because it is superseded'
payload.decision: Recall kept the newer memory for Sam from Goldman and excluded the older one because it was marked superseded, so the current result reflects the latest unsuperseded information.

entity_resolution.reason: Sam is referenced only by name in the fixture, so no durable entity ID can be confirmed from the provided input.

payload.intent: Explain why recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman and filtered mem_old because it is superseded

memory_cards.statement: mem_new is the active memory for Sam from Goldman, and it supersedes the older record.
mem_old is an older memory about Sam from Goldman that was filtered out because it has been superseded.

details: Identified supersession as the conflict type.
Explained why mem_new was selected and mem_old was filtered.
Preserved ambiguity about Sam's durable identity.

repair_options: Retain mem_new as current and suppress mem_old in recall results.
If both must be shown, label mem_old explicitly as superseded.
Avoid committing any additional identity details for Sam unless supported by stronger evidence.
